jd_text:
Job Title: Senior Merchandiser -- Women's Wear (Jersey)
Company: H&M Group
Location: Bengaluru, India
Employment Type: Full-Time
Experience Level: 4-6 years

ABOUT THE ROLE
As a Senior Merchandiser in our Women's Wear Jersey team, you will drive range planning and
assortment strategy for one of our key product categories. You will work closely with the buying team,
designers, and regional teams to ensure the right product is in the right place at the right time -- at
the right price.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
- Own the OTB (Open-to-Buy) planning and management for the women's jersey category, ensuring
  optimal stock levels across channels and geographies
- Lead assortment planning for seasonal and replenishment ranges, balancing trend, commercial
  viability, and brand positioning
- Analyze weekly sales performance, sell-through rates, and margin data to make data-driven
  decisions on replenishment, markdowns, and range adjustments
- Collaborate with the buying team on range development, identifying trends and translating them
  into commercial assortment plans
- Manage supplier/vendor relationships, coordinating on production timelines, MOQs, and delivery
  schedules to ensure 95%+ on-time delivery
- Partner with regional teams (India, SEA) to understand local market needs and adapt assortment
  accordingly
- Drive category P&L performance by managing margins, stock turn, and sell-through KPIs
- Lead product lifecycle management (PLM) from range development through end-of-season clearance
- Conduct range reviews with cross-functional teams including design, marketing, and VM

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR
- 4-6 years of experience in fashion buying, merchandising, or category management
- Demonstrated experience with OTB management and assortment planning
- Strong analytical skills -- Advanced Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, data dashboards), experience
  with Metabase, Power BI, or similar BI tools is a plus
- Experience managing PO/PI coordination with multiple vendors and factories
- Understanding of womenswear trends, especially in the jersey/casualwear category
- Proven ability to manage multiple suppliers and factories across India and Asia
- Experience in both offline retail and e-commerce/omnichannel merchandising
- Familiarity with SAP or other ERP systems (PO/inventory tracking)
- Strong cross-functional collaboration skills -- you will work closely with buying, design, VM,
  marketing, and supply chain teams
- Excellent negotiation skills with track record of improving vendor pricing and MOQs

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
- NIFT, Pearl Academy, or equivalent fashion institute qualification
- Experience with fast-fashion or value retail (knowledge of DMart, Zara, Zudio, Mango assortment
  strategies is a plus)
- Exposure to category launch or new market entry projects
- Experience building and operating WIP trackers and production calendars
master_cv:
VARSHA SATHYANARAYANAN
thisisvarshasathya@gmail.com | +91 8939432182 | www.linkedin.com/in/varshasathya/
SUMMARY
Fashion professional with 3+ years of experience in buying, merchandising, category management, and P&L management
across retail and e-commerce. Experienced in assortment planning, vendor sourcing, PO/PI coordination, and product
lifecycle execution for apparel categories including bottom-wear, casualwear, and accessories. Proven ability to analyze
sales performance, optimize assortments, negotiate supplier terms, and improve margins and sell-through across multi-
channel retail environments.
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
SuperK Bangalore, India
Category Manager – Fashion Nov 2025 – Present
Built the fashion category from near zero across online channels and a tech-enabled retail network of 3+ coco stores,
launching assortments across apparel, footwear, and accessories while driving vendor expansion, category P&L
management, and data-led merchandising decisions.
• Expanded the fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories across menswear, womenswear, kidswear,
footwear, and accessories by onboarding 12+ regional vendors, identifying assortment gaps, and benchmarking
category offerings against value retail players like DMart and Vishal Mega Mart.
• Analyzed weekly sales performance, pricing benchmarks, and customer demand signals thereby generating ₹ 3.5
lakh in category revenue within two months.
• Negotiated vendor pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms, improving product margins from 22% to 35% while
maintaining competitive retail pricing.
• Partnered with marketing teams to launch fashion-led campaigns during New Year, Pongal and Republic Day
increasing category visibility by ~22% and driving ~15% uplift in campaign period sales.
• Collaborated with operations and supply chain teams to improve stock availability for fast-moving SKUs,
coordinating warehouse invoicing and inventory flow to reduce stock-outs by ~20% across priority products.
• Utilized Excel and Metabase dashboards for tracking sell-through, AOV, and margin performance; identified
underperforming SKUs and implemented assortment rationalization that improved category gross margin by ~6%.
The Style Gram Founder Remote - Chennai, India
Nov 2024 – Jun 2025
Built and operated a lean fashion reselling brand focused on affordable women’s western-wear, managing end-to-end
sourcing, merchandising, content creation, and customer engagement across Instagram and Swiggy Minis.
• Generated ₹50,000 in revenue during the launch phase with an average order value of ₹600, fulfilling 80+
customer orders across Tier 1 and Tier 2 cities with zero product returns.
• Sourced and merchandised 60+ SKUs across tops, skirts, and dresses through direct supplier negotiations,
maintaining 45% gross margins and reducing sourcing turnaround time by 25%.
• Evaluated and engaged 5 supplier partners before finalizing a primary vendor to optimize pricing, quality
consistency, and procurement turnaround time.
• Executed pricing, merchandising, and inventory tracking through Excel-based systems, ensuring 100% order
fulfillment and streamlined stock management.
• Managed 60+ SKUs on Swiggy Minis and built a custom Excel-based WIP tracker to manage catalog listings,
order tracking, and inventory visibility, reducing catalog errors by 90% and improving stock flow for fast-selling
styles.
• Produced 25+ branded content assets and launched 3+ Instagram ad campaigns, improving click-through rates
by 22% through A/B testing and targeted messaging.
Go Colors (Go Fashion India Ltd.) Assistant Fashion Manager Chennai, India
Sept 2020 – June 2023
Supported buying and merchandising for women’s and girls’ bottom-wear categories including leggings, track pants, formal
pants, ethnic bottoms, and shorts, with responsibility for vendor coordination, product lifecycle execution, and data-led
assortment decisions.
• Managed PO/PI coordination and vendor communication across 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories across
India and Asia, ensuring 95% on-time delivery for seasonal production orders.
• Analyzed weekly sales performance for 200+ SKUs, recommending replenishment and assortment adjustments
that improved category sell-through by 15%.
• Aligned assortment planning with trend insights and performance data, driving a 10% lift in customer purchase
rate across key bottom-wear categories.
• Launched 3+ new sub-categories including kids-wear, leading a 2-member team to introduce new styles and
revamp existing collections, boosting category sales by 15% in the first quarter.
• Negotiated supplier pricing and MOQs during fabric sourcing and production planning, maintaining product
quality while ensuring competitive margins.
• Led sampling, fit approvals, and production coordination for 30+ styles per season, reducing post-launch return
rates to under 10% while gaining exposure to PLM-driven product development workflows.
• Built WIP trackers to monitor production timelines and collaborated with logistics, VM, and marketing teams to
ensure timely product launches.
EDUCATION
Humber College - Longo School of Business Graduate Certificate in Fashion Management National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT) Bachelor of Design - Fashion Design Toronto, Canada
Sept 2023 – Aug 2024
Chennai, India
July 2016 – July 2020
SKILLS
Core Competencies: Assortment Planning • Fashion Buying • PO/PI Coordination • Vendor Management • Inventory
Control • Forecasting • TNA Calendar Tracking • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) • Order Tracking
Tools: Advanced MS Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Dashboards) • MS Word • Google Sheets • Canva • Meta Ads
Manager • SAP (PO/Inventory Tracking – Basic)
Functional Strengths: Cross-Functional Collaboration • Trend Analysis • Sales Performance Reporting • Catalog & Content
Management • E-commerce Marketing • Vendor Negotiation

less experience with BI tools like Metabase/Power BI

risks_to_soften: lack of direct jersey category experience
less experience with BI tools like Metabase/Power BI
s7_rewrite_context.max_5_bullet_guidance: Lead every bullet with a strong action verb and a measurable outcome. Replace weak verbs (Analyzed, Supported, Assisted) with ownership verbs (Built, Owned, Shipped, Drove). Keep all metrics exact.

unknowns: specific culture details beyond JD tone
current team size or reporting structure

nice_to_haves: WIP tracker and production calendar experience
hidden_expectations: P&L ownership and profit-driven decision making
range review leadership across design, marketing, VM
day_one_deliverables: Assume OTB control for women's jersey category
Analyze current sell-through and stock health
Initiate assortment planning for upcoming season

confirmed_skills.evidence: Planned and expanded assortments across roles, including launching sub-categories and rationalizing SKUs to improve margin.
Negotiated pricing and terms with vendors, improving product margins from 22% to 35% and optimizing sourcing terms.
Built WIP trackers, dashboards, and systems for pricing, merchandising, and inventory tracking across all roles.
Managed PO/PI coordination for 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories, ensuring 95% on-time delivery.
Onboarded and coordinated 12+ regional vendors and multiple factories across India and Asia.
Collaborated with marketing, operations, supply chain, VM, and logistics for product launches and campaigns.
Experience in women’s bottom-wear, casualwear, and accessories with trend-informed assortment planning.
Managed category P&L at SuperK, driving margin improvement and revenue generation.
Tracked inventory, reduced stock-outs by 20%, and streamlined stock management using Excel and Metabase.
Used Metabase dashboards for tracking sell-through, AOV, and margin performance at SuperK.

Assortment Planning: Expanded fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories
Analyzed weekly sales data to optimize assortments, generating revenue
Recommended assortment adjustments improving sell-through by 15%
Launched 3+ sub-categories at Go Colors, boosting sales
Vendor Negotiation: Negotiated vendor pricing, improving margins from 22% to 35%
Negotiated MOQs and sourcing terms for better margins
Evaluated and engaged 5 supplier partners before finalizing primary vendor
Advanced Excel: Built Excel-based WIP trackers for 60+ SKUs, reducing catalog errors by 90%
Used Excel and Metabase dashboards for tracking sell-through, AOV, margin
Implemented Excel-based systems for pricing, merchandising, inventory tracking
Vendor and Factory Coordination (PO/PI): Managed PO/PI coordination for 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories
Ensured 95% on-time delivery for seasonal production orders
Multi-Supplier Management (India/Asia): Onboarded 12+ regional vendors for fashion category expansion
Coordinated with suppliers and factories across India and Asia
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partnered with marketing, operations, supply chain, VM, and logistics
Collaborated for product launches, campaigns, and stock availability
Womenswear Buying & Trends: Managed buying for women’s bottom-wear: leggings, track pants, formal pants, ethnic bottoms
Aligned assortment with trend insights, driving purchase rate lift
P&L Management: Managed category P&L at SuperK, improving gross margin by ~6%
Generated ₹3.5 lakh category revenue in two months
Inventory Management: Reduced stock-outs by ~20% across priority products
Managed 60+ SKUs with 100% order fulfillment and inventory tracking
BI Tools (Metabase): Utilized Metabase dashboards for sell-through, AOV, and margin performance tracking

strongest_proof_points: Built fashion category from near zero, generating ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months at SuperK.
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiation and pricing strategy.
Managed PO/PI coordination for 20+ suppliers across India and Asia, achieving 95% on-time delivery at Go Colors.
Launched 3+ sub-categories including kids-wear, boosting category sales by 15% at Go Colors.
Reduced stock-outs by ~20% through cross-functional collaboration on inventory flow at SuperK.
claims_allowed: 4+ years of experience in fashion buying and merchandising across retail and e-commerce.
Proven ability to manage P&L and drive margin improvement.
Expert in assortment planning and vendor negotiation.
Strong analytical skills with advanced Excel and BI tools (Metabase).
Omnichannel experience with offline retail and online sales channels.
Multi-supplier management across India and Asia.
NIFT graduate with fashion design background.
Experience launching new categories and sub-categories.
Proficient in WIP tracking and production coordination.
Collaborated cross-functionally with marketing, operations, and supply chain.
claims_not_allowed: Proficient in SAP for merchandising operations.
Deep expertise in jersey fabrics and casualwear.
Managed large teams of buyers and planners.

positioning_strategy.one_line_positioning: P&L-driven Senior Merchandiser scaling womenswear categories from zero and lifting margins 13 percentage points, ready to drive the women's jersey business at H&M India.
positioning_strategy.narrative_angle: A NIFT-trained merchandiser who built a fashion category from near zero to ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months while improving margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiation—bringing commercial acumen and multi-supplier coordination across India and Asia. While jersey-specific experience is developing, a proven record of launching new categories and rapid P&L ownership shows readiness to master the category fast.
lead_strengths: Built fashion category from near zero, generating ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months at SuperK.
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiation and pricing strategy.
Managed PO/PI coordination for 20+ suppliers across India and Asia, achieving 95% on-time delivery at Go Colors.
proof_points_to_emphasize: Expanded fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories
Negotiated vendor pricing, improving margins from 22% to 35%
Managed PO/PI coordination for 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories
Reduced stock-outs by ~20% across priority products
Launched 3+ sub-categories at Go Colors, boosting sales
things_to_downplay: Limited exposure to SAP or similar ERP systems
Jersey/casualwear expertise: experience is more in bottom-wear and accessories, not pure jersey
Omnichannel experience is indirect, not explicitly led across offline and online
positioning_strategy.tone_guidance: Lead with commercial impact and vendor relationships. Use retail terminology (sell-through, margin, OTB cautiously). Frame lack of jersey as a quick learning curve given NIFT background and womenswear expertise. Emphasize P&L ownership and speed. Avoid overpromising on SAP.
positioning_strategy.recruiter_first_impression_target: This candidate has the core merchandising chops and vendor network to manage a category, just needs ramp-up on H&M-specific tools and jersey fabric knowledge.
positioning_strategy.reasoning: Differentiator is entrepreneurial P&L ownership with strong vendor negotiation and multi-supplier coordination in the Indian context, aligning with the role’s need to drive profitability and manage suppliers. Gaps in OTB, SAP, and specific jersey category create risk, but transferable womenswear skills and proven commercial acumen make this a strong stretch candidate. CAREFUL_PUSH stance acknowledges the fit while being transparent about ramp-up.

summary.original_text:
Fashion professional with 3+ years of experience in buying, merchandising, category management, and P&L management
across retail and e-commerce. Experienced in assortment planning, vendor sourcing, PO/PI coordination, and product
lifecycle execution for apparel categories including bottom-wear, casualwear, and accessories. Proven ability to analyze
sales performance, optimize assortments, negotiate supplier terms, and improve margins and sell-through across multi-
channel retail environments.
summary.rewritten_text:
NIFT-trained merchandiser with 4+ years of experience driving P&L, assortment planning, and vendor negotiation across retail and e-commerce. Built a fashion category from near zero to ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months while improving margins from 22% to 35% through direct supplier negotiations—demonstrating readiness to own OTB and sell-through for women's jersey.

Proven ability to manage multi-supplier coordination across India and Asia, achieving 95% on-time delivery for 20+ vendors. Launched 3+ sub-categories including kids-wear, boosting category sales by 15%. Reduced stock-outs by ~20% through cross-functional collaboration on inventory flow.

Expert in assortment planning and advanced Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, dashboards) with omnichannel experience spanning offline retail and online sales. While jersey-specific expertise is developing, a track record of rapidly mastering new categories and delivering commercial impact positions me to excel in fast-fashion cycles.
summary.change_reason: Reframed summary to lead with commercial outcomes and vendor negotiation, inject OTB/sell-through language, and address jersey gap as a quick learning curve.

skills.rewritten_text:
- OTB & Assortment Planning: Built fashion category from near zero to ₹3.5L revenue in 2 months; launched 3+ sub-categories boosting sales 15%.
- Vendor Negotiation & Margin Management: Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through pricing strategy and supplier negotiations.
- Advanced Excel & BI: Proficient in pivot tables, VLOOKUP, dashboards; used Metabase for sell-through and stock health analysis.
- Omnichannel Coordination: Managed PO/PI for 20+ suppliers across India/Asia, achieving 95% on-time delivery; reduced stock-outs ~20% via cross-functional inventory flow.
- Fast Fashion Adaptability: NIFT-trained with womenswear expertise; quick learner for jersey/casualwear with proven category launch speed.
skills.change_reason: Reframed skills to lead with OTB, assortment planning, and vendor negotiation—core must-haves—while downplaying lack of jersey experience and emphasizing commercial outcomes and fast learning.

- Launched 3+ new sub-categories including kids-wear, leading a 2-member team to introduce new styles and revamp existing collections, boosting category sales by 15% in the first quarter.
- Negotiated supplier pricing and MOQs during fabric sourcing and production planning, maintaining product quality while ensuring competitive margins.
- Led sampling, fit approvals, and production coordination for 30+ styles per season, reducing post-launch return rates to under 10% while gaining exposure to PLM-driven product development workflows.
- Built WIP trackers to monitor production timelines and collaborated with logistics, VM, and marketing teams to ensure timely product launches.
